issue.author: JonPsson

comments.author: JonPsson
comments.body:
(-target s390x-linux-gnu)
Eventually the problem becomes clear as a constant '5' is loaded just before printf before return...

comments.author: nikic
JonPsson
comments.body: It's not in the backend - see previous comment about the constant '5' being loaded before isel.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
Oh yeah, you're right. So alive just misses this.

This is the IR before the relevant LICM run: https://gist.github.com/nikic/f60e97976eab9d73ccdb5d9e12c9b55e

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
A bit further reduced: https://gist.github.com/nikic/d4412c2289144a70168b9bfe9b784308

For some reason, SafetyInfo believes that the store to `@c` is guaranteed to execute.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
A further reduction:
```
@c = global i16 0, align 2
 
define void @test() {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %v = phi i32 [ 10, %entry ], [ 0, %loop.latch ]
  %c = icmp eq i32 %v, 0
  br i1 %c, label %loop.exit, label %loop.cont

loop.cont:                                        ; preds = %loop
  br i1 false, label %loop.irreducible, label %loop.latch

loop.irreducible:                                 ; preds = %loop.latch, %loop.cont
  store i16 5, ptr @c, align 2
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.irreducible, %loop.cont
  br i1 false, label %loop.irreducible, label %loop

loop.exit:                                        ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}
```

This one has an even more obvious failure mode, because the store gets hoisted into the entry block.

Clearly LoopSafetyInfo is broken in the presence of irreducible control flow :(

comments.author: kasperk81
comments.body: i'm guessing it's a potential candidate for backporting to 15.x?

issue.title: [LICM] wrongcode at -O2

patch:
commit 884bb97dca257b90dbe6fd0faf09512858292151
Author: Nikita Popov <npopov@redhat.com>
Date:   Tue Sep 20 13:04:27 2022 +0200

    [MustExec][LICM] Handle latch being part of an inner cycle (PR57780)
    
    The algorithm in allLoopPathsLeadToBlock() does not handle the case
    where the loop latch is part of the predecessor set correctly: In
    this case, we may take the backedge (escaping to a different loop
    iteration) and not execute other latch successors. This can happen
    if the latch is part of an inner cycle.
    
    Fixes https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/57780.
    
    Differential Revision: https://reviews.llvm.org/D134279

diff --git a/llvm/lib/Analysis/MustExecute.cpp b/llvm/lib/Analysis/MustExecute.cpp
index 6296f5805157..8a6318eabecf 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Analysis/MustExecute.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Analysis/MustExecute.cpp
@@ -201,6 +201,15 @@ bool LoopSafetyInfo::allLoopPathsLeadToBlock(const Loop *CurLoop,
   SmallPtrSet<const BasicBlock *, 4> Predecessors;
   collectTransitivePredecessors(CurLoop, BB, Predecessors);
 
+  // Bail out if a latch block is part of the predecessor set. In this case
+  // we may take the backedge to the header and not execute other latch
+  // successors.
+  for (const BasicBlock *Pred : predecessors(CurLoop->getHeader()))
+    // Predecessors only contains loop blocks, so we don't have to worry about
+    // preheader predecessors here.
+    if (Predecessors.contains(Pred))
+      return false;
+
   // Make sure that all successors of, all predecessors of BB which are not
   // dominated by BB, are either:
   // 1) BB,

tests.test_body:
@c = external global i16, align 2

define void @latch_cycle_reducible() {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %v = phi i32 [ 10, %entry ], [ 0, %loop.latch ]
  %c = icmp eq i32 %v, 0
  br i1 %c, label %loop.exit, label %loop2

loop2:                                            ; preds = %loop.latch, %loop
  br i1 false, label %loop2.cont, label %loop.latch

loop2.cont:                                       ; preds = %loop2
  store i16 5, ptr @c, align 2
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop2.cont, %loop2
  br i1 false, label %loop2, label %loop

loop.exit:                                        ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

@c = external global i16, align 2

define void @latch_cycle_irreducible() {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %v = phi i32 [ 10, %entry ], [ 0, %loop.latch ]
  %c = icmp eq i32 %v, 0
  br i1 %c, label %loop.exit, label %loop.cont

loop.cont:                                        ; preds = %loop
  br i1 false, label %loop.irreducible, label %loop.latch

loop.irreducible:                                 ; preds = %loop.latch, %loop.cont
  store i16 5, ptr @c, align 2
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.irreducible, %loop.cont
  br i1 false, label %loop.irreducible, label %loop

loop.exit:                                        ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

@c = external global i16, align 2

define void @test() {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %v = phi i32 [ 10, %entry ], [ 0, %loop.latch ]
  %c = icmp eq i32 %v, 0
  br i1 %c, label %loop.exit, label %loop.cont

loop.cont:                                        ; preds = %loop
  br i1 false, label %loop.irreducible, label %loop.latch

loop.irreducible:                                 ; preds = %loop.latch, %loop.cont
  store i16 5, ptr @c, align 2
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop.irreducible, %loop.cont
  br i1 false, label %loop.irreducible, label %loop

loop.exit:                                        ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}
